description_long: This is a two-story log cabin with a dark, multi-hipped roof and prominent overhangs, constructed primarily from horizontal wooden logs. The structure features a stone foundation and several covered porches, with wooden railings and posts supporting the roof extensions. Multiple rectangular windows are distributed across the facades, some grouped in pairs, providing natural light to the interior. The main entrance is sheltered by a deep porch, while a garage door is visible on one side, indicating vehicular access. The overall design is robust and rustic, blending traditional log construction with modern residential amenities. The cabin's exterior is dominated by natural wood tones and dark roofing, giving it a warm, inviting appearance suitable for suburban or park-like urban settings.
description: A large, two-story log cabin with a dark hipped roof, stone foundation, covered porches, and multiple windows. The structure combines rustic wood construction with modern residential features like a garage.
description_view_0: This view shows the main entrance porch, garage door, and a prominent overhanging roof, highlighting the front facade and entryway.
description_view_1: This view displays the rear of the cabin with a wide roof overhang, a central upper window, and symmetrical window placement on the lower level.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the depth of the porch, the side windows, and the extension of the roof over the entry steps.
description_view_3: This view reveals the back and side of the cabin, with grouped windows and a secondary covered porch area.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with multiple intersecting hips, deep overhangs, and dark shingle-like material
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows of medium size, arranged symmetrically on the rear and sides, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced individually; no large or floor-to-ceiling windows visible
attribute_door_placement: One main entrance door centered under the front porch, and a large garage door on the right side of the front facade; no other exterior doors visible
